items.title: The Relationship Between Sleep, Cognition, and Dementia Risk in People With Focal Epilepsy.
items.abstract: Sleep disruption and cognitive impairment are important comorbidities of focal epilepsy. However, the nature to which sleep affects cognition and long-term dementia risk in focal epilepsy, compared with other brain conditions, remains unclear. We examined the relationship between sleep, cognition, and dementia risk in patients with focal epilepsy compared with healthy controls and stroke patients. We conducted an analysis of the prospective UK Biobank cohort study, with baseline assessments performed between 2006 and 2010 and follow-up until 2021. Study groups were mutually exclusive participants with focal epilepsy and stroke at baseline assessment and healthy controls. Sleep characteristics included reports of sleep duration, obstructive sleep apnea, insomnia, napping, and dozing. Main outcomes were risk of incident all-cause dementia and Alzheimer disease from Cox proportional hazard modeling and comparison of executive function measures and brain total hippocampal and gray matter volumes using generalized linear modeling. We examined a sample of 482,207 participants, aged between 38 and 72 years (mean [SD] 57.6 [8.1] years; 53.8% female), without dementia at baseline and a nested imaging subsample of 42,345 participants. Optimal sleep duration (6-8 hours) was associated with better executive function in control, focal epilepsy, and stroke groups. The impact of optimal sleep was significantly higher in individuals with focal epilepsy compared with controls (interaction term,  Optimal sleep modified both cognitive performance and dementia risk in individuals with focal epilepsy compared with stroke patients and healthy controls. Based on self-reported sleep data, these findings suggest that improving sleep may be an impactful intervention to improve cognition and reduce dementia risk particularly in focal epilepsy.
items.summary_ja:
背景：局所てんかん患者では睡眠障害と認知機能障害が重要な併存症であるが、睡眠が認知機能や長期的な認知症リスクに与える影響は他の脳疾患と比較して不明である。  
方法：2006～2010年に英国バイオバンクで実施された前向きコホート研究を用い、局所てんかん患者、脳卒中患者、健常者の睡眠特性（睡眠時間、閉塞性睡眠時無呼吸、不眠、昼寝、居眠り）と認知機能、認知症発症リスクの関連を解析した。対象は38～72歳の482,207名（うち42,345名は脳画像データあり）。  
結果：最適な睡眠時間（6～8時間）は全群で実行機能の向上と関連し、局所てんかん群でその効果が健常者より有意に大きかった。最適睡眠は認知機能だけでなく認知症リスクも修飾し、局所てんかん患者において特に顕著であった。  
結論：自己申告による睡眠改善は、局所てんかん患者の認知機能向上および認知症リスク低減に有効な介入となり得る。
items.summary_short_ja: 焦点てんかん患者において、睡眠の質と認知機能、認知症リスクの関係をUKバイオバンクの大規模コホートで検討した。最適な睡眠時間（6～8時間）は認知機能の向上と認知症リスク低減に関連し、特に焦点てんかん患者でその効果が顕著であった。これらの結果は、睡眠改善が焦点てんかん患者の認知機能維持と認知症予防に有効な介入となり得ることを示唆している。

items.title: Virtual vs In-Person Neurologic Ambulatory Care: A Case-Control Study of Subsequent Health Care Utilization.
items.abstract: Implementation of telemedicine expanded options for outpatient neurology care. It remains uncertain which new neurology patients can be appropriately evaluated virtually. We compared subsequent health care utilization after virtual vs in-person new patient neurology visits across 3 academic medical centers. We conducted a retrospective multicenter cohort study of adults with a new outpatient neurology visit from September 2020 through December 2021 using the Vizient Clinical Data Base and Clinical Practice Solutions Center databases. Virtual and in-person patients were matched 1:1 using propensity scores incorporating demographics, clinical characteristics, time period, and previous health care utilization. Outcomes were analyzed overall and stratified by neurologic chief complaint category and institution. We compared rates of subsequent neurologic clinic follow-up, emergency department (ED) visits, and hospitalizations after virtual and in-person encounters. Testing and all-cause ED visits/hospitalizations were also assessed. We identified 10,428 virtual and 36,767 in-person neurology new outpatient visits. After propensity score matching, 8,202 virtual visits were matched to 8,202 in-person visits. Neurology follow-up within 90 days did not differ between virtual and in-person visits (24.6% vs 23.7%,  In this propensity score-matched multicenter cohort, new neurology patients seen virtually had similar downstream utilization as those seen in-person, including comparable 90-day follow-up and similar neurologic and all-cause ED visits and hospitalizations. Although follow-up varied modestly by chief complaint and testing was more frequent after some in-person visits, no major differences emerged overall.
items.summary_ja:
背景：遠隔医療の導入により、神経内科の外来診療の選択肢が拡大したが、新規患者のうちどの症例が適切に遠隔診療で評価可能かは不明である。  
方法：2020年9月から2021年12月までの3つの大学病院における新規神経内科外来患者を対象に、遠隔診療と対面診療の患者を傾向スコアマッチングで1対1にマッチさせ、診療後の医療利用状況を後ろ向きに比較した。主な評価項目は90日以内の神経内科再診、救急受診、入院の発生率である。  
結果：10,428件の遠隔診療と36,767件の対面診療のうち、8,202件ずつをマッチング。90日以内の神経内科再診率は遠隔24.6％、対面23.7％で差はなかった。救急受診や入院率も両群で類似していた。主訴別や施設別で若干の差はあったが、全体として大きな違いは認められなかった。  
結論：新規神経内科患者において、遠隔診療は対面診療と比較してその後の医療利用に大きな差がなく、適切な診療形態の一つとして有効である可能性が示された。
items.summary_short_ja: 本研究は、3つの大学病院で新規神経内科外来患者のバーチャル診療と対面診療後の医療利用を比較した。傾向スコアマッチングにより8202例ずつ比較した結果、90日以内の神経内科再診率や救急受診・入院率に有意差はなかった。主訴別や検査頻度に若干の差はあったが、全体としてバーチャル診療は対面診療と同等の医療利用パターンを示した。
